author: Patrick Buckley
message:
Bump MSRV to 1.88, upgrade ratatui 0.30 and crossterm 0.29 (#61)

Resolves two Dependabot security alerts:
- time 0.3.45 → 0.3.47 (stack exhaustion DoS via RFC 2822)
- lru 0.12.5 → 0.16.3 (Stacked Borrows soundness in IterMut)

Collapse 28 nested if/if-let blocks into let-chains, which are
stable since Rust 1.87 and now flagged by clippy.

author: Patrick Buckley
message:
Fix invalid pointer dereference in network collector (#60)

* Fix invalid pointer dereference in network collector

Use ptr::read() instead of &* to avoid creating references from raw
pointers into getifaddrs-owned memory. Add null check on ifa_name
before CStr::from_ptr. Classify IPv6 scope by address prefix (FE80::/10)
instead of relying on sin6_scope_id zone index.

* fix: use reference with inline CodeQL suppression instead of ptr::read

Revert to &* reference style to collapse 6 CodeQL findings back to 1,
then suppress that single finding with an inline codeql[] comment citing
the getifaddrs/freeifaddrs safety contract. Keeps the null checks on
ifa_name/ifa_addr and the ipv6_scope() address-based classification.

author: Patrick Buckley
message:
Add least-privilege permissions to CI and AUR workflows (#59)

Fixes 7 CodeQL alerts for missing permissions declarations.
Both workflows only need contents:read — CI for checkout+build,
AUR for checkout (push uses SSH secrets, not GITHUB_TOKEN).

author: Ewout van Mansom
message: Package shell completion (#58)

author: weriomat
message:
Add shell completion scripts (#57)

This is done via the amazing [cargo
xtask](https://github.com/matklad/cargo-xtask) paradigm.
Another possible solution would be to add "shell completions" as an
option to `Commands`. As shell completions are only useful when
packaging an application I prefer the xtask approach.

author: Ewout van Mansom
message:
Userspace sensor permissions via udev and setcap (#51)

Ship udev rules, tmpfiles.d config, and file capabilities so
all sensors work without root for logged-in users.

udev rules (packaging/udev/50-siomon.rules):
- TAG+="uaccess" on /dev/hsmp, /dev/ipmi*, /dev/i2c-*,
  /dev/port, /dev/sinfo_io — systemd-logind sets POSIX
  ACLs for the active console user, no group needed

tmpfiles.d (packaging/tmpfiles/siomon.conf):
- RAPL energy counters (/sys/class/powercap/) are 0400
  root on many distros — make world-readable at boot
  via systemd-tmpfiles z directive

File capabilities (post-install hooks):
- setcap cap_perfmon,cap_sys_rawio=ep on /usr/bin/sio
- cap_perfmon: perf_event_open for L3 cache hit rate
- cap_sys_rawio: /dev/port for Super I/O chips

AUR (packaging/aur/):
- PKGBUILD installs udev rules and tmpfiles.d config
- siomon.install runs setcap in post_install; udev and
  tmpfiles are handled by pre-existing libalpm hooks
- libcap added to depends for setcap availability

Debian/PPA (packaging/launchpad/debian/):
- siomon.udev and siomon.tmpfiles copied from canonical
  sources during CI build for debhelper auto-install
- postinst runs setcap; udev and tmpfiles are handled
  by dh_installudev and dh_installtmpfiles via DEBHELPER
- override_dh_fixperms re-applies caps after strip
- libcap2-bin added to Build-Depends and Depends

CI workflows:
- publish-aur.yml copies siomon.install to builder and
  pushes it to AUR alongside PKGBUILD and .SRCINFO
- publish-ppa.yml copies canonical udev/tmpfiles into
  debian/ at build time; sed pattern narrowed to not
  break with added libcap2-bin in Build-Depends

author: Patrick Buckley
message:
Add 30 board templates with sensor labels and voltage scaling (#54)

* Add 30 board templates with sensor labels and voltage scaling

MSI (new vendor, 4 boards):
- B350 Tomahawk, X370 SLI Plus, X470 GAMING PRO (NCT6795)
- B450M Mortar (NCT6797)

ASRock (7 boards):
- AB350 Pro4, X370 Taichi, X370 Gaming K4 (NCT6779)
- B450 Gaming-ITX (NCT6792), A300M DeskMini (NCT6793)
- Z390 Extreme4 (NCT6791), Z390M-ITX (NCT6793)

ASUS (8 boards):
- TUF X570-PLUS (NCT6798), PRIME Z370-A (NCT6793)
- H87-PRO, P10S-M WS (NCT6791)
- P8P67 PRO, P8Z68-V LX, Q1900-ITX (NCT6776)
- P8B75-V (NCT6779)

Gigabyte (11 boards):
- AX370-GAMING 5, AB350-GAMING 3, AB350N-Gaming WIFI,
  AX370M-DS3H, X470 ULTRA GAMING (IT8686 + IT8792)
- Z77-D3H, B75-D3V, H67MA-UD2H (IT8728)
- H170M-D3H (IT8628), F2A88XM-HD3 (IT8620)
- GA-870A-UD3 (IT8720)

Adds shared base label constants for MSI NCT6795, Gigabyte IT8686,
and Gigabyte IT8728 chip families. All voltage scaling factors are
verified against known resistor divider networks.

* Address review feedback: tighten matching, improve docs and tests

- TUF X570: add "plus" to match_substrings to avoid matching X570-PRO
- Clarify shared constant doc comments as defaults/baselines, noting
  that boards with different divider networks override inline
- Fix ambiguity test to actually count matching templates per board
  name instead of just checking first-match exists
- Remove phase/batch references from test comments

author: Patrick Buckley
message:
Address AUR release workflow review feedback (#42)

Apply least-privilege permissions and explicit inputs to the publish-aur
job in the release pipeline, addressing Copilot review feedback from #35.

Add reference comments to PKGBUILD and publish-aur workflow so future
reviewers can verify design decisions (host-tuple target specifier, empty
build(), ssh-keyscan TOFU) against upstream documentation rather than
re-flagging them.

author: Patrick Buckley
message:
Feature/ite boards voltage scaling (#53)

* Add ITE IT8613/IT8628/IT8655/IT8665/IT8695/IT8696/IT8792 chip support

Extend Super I/O chip detection and direct-IO sensor reading for 7 new
ITE chip families commonly found on Gigabyte and ASUS motherboards.

Detection: add device IDs including non-obvious IT8792E (0x8733) and
IT87952E (0x8695).

Direct-IO: per-chip ADC resolution (10.9/11/12 mV), per-chip fan/temp
channel counts, register 0x77 check for shared temp/voltage registers,
IT8665 fan 6 alternate register address, IT8613 fan 1 skip.

* Add hwmon voltage scaling, board templates, and vendor matching

Add HwmonConfig struct with voltage_scaling field to BoardTemplate for
correcting hwmon voltage readings with external resistor divider
multipliers. Thread scaling from config through poller to hwmon source.
Users can override via [voltage_scaling] in config.toml.

Add 17 new board templates with sensor labels and voltage scaling:
- Gigabyte X870 (4 boards), X570 (2), B550 (2), B450 (2), B650M,
  TRX40, Z690 with ITE chip labels from hardware documentation
- ASUS Prime B350/B450 with IT8655/IT8665 labels
- Beelink EQ/SEi mini-PCs with IT8613 labels
- Shared label/scaling constants to reduce duplication

Add match_vendor field to BoardTemplate (replaces match_any) for safer
board matching. Beelink boards require vendor "azw" to avoid false
positives from their short DMI names ("EQ", "SEi"). ASUS X670E boards
with OR-logic matching split into paired statics.

Add hwmon voltage scaling to existing NCT6798/6799 boards (WRX90E,
ASRock WRX90, X670E family) using multipliers validated against
LibreHardwareMonitor Ri/Rf resistor divider values.

Fix voltage_scaling.rs divider ratio comments (5:1 not 4:1 for +5V).

https://github.com/level1techs/siomon/issues/33

* Address review feedback: tests, allocations, formatting

- Fix test_no_ambiguous_matches to use lookup_board_with_vendor instead
  of reimplementing matching logic without vendor check
- Add unit tests for load_voltage_scaling (board resolution, user
  override, unknown board)
- Remove Vec allocation in read_fans hot path by iterating the chain
  directly instead of collecting
- Format VOLTAGE_REGS_BASE as multi-line for consistency

author: Patrick Buckley
message:
Disambiguate hwmon chips with duplicate names (#33) (#52)

* Disambiguate hwmon chips with duplicate names (#33)

When multiple hwmon sysfs devices share the same name (e.g. jc42 DIMM
temp sensors, multiple NVMe drives), they produced identical SensorIds
and collided in the HashMap — only one reading survived.

Detect duplicate chip names during discovery and append the sysfs device
symlink basename to disambiguate (e.g. jc42-9-0018, nvme-nvme0). Falls
back to the hwmon directory index when no device symlink exists.

Also fixes is_gpu_hwmon_chip to use prefix matching so multi-GPU systems
still get "GPU " label prefixes, and pre-expands board template label
overrides so unqualified names continue to match disambiguated chips.

https://github.com/level1techs/siomon/issues/33

* Address review feedback on hwmon disambiguation

- Avoid allocation in is_gpu_hwmon_chip by using strip_prefix instead
  of format!
- Fix curr sensor discovery passing original label_overrides instead
  of effective_overrides
- Extract expand_label_overrides as a pure testable function
- Add unit tests for label override expansion (no duplicates, with
  duplicates, qualified precedence)

author: Patrick Buckley
message:
Harden SPD page-0 restore and add self-healing to DDR5 temp poller (#49)

* Harden SPD page-0 restore and add self-healing to DDR5 temp poller

SPD EEPROM reader: replace 2-attempt page restore with 5-attempt
exponential backoff (0/1/5/10/50ms). Verify restoration by reading
MR11 back — MR0 can't verify page state since it's in the management
register range and always returns 0x51 regardless of page.

DDR5 temp poller: reset MR11 to page 0 before each hub temperature
read. This self-heals from a failed page restore without requiring a
reboot. Only hub sensors (SPD5118 at 0x50-0x53) have page select;
TS0/TS1 are unaffected. MR11 is volatile — no EEPROM wear.

Race condition: move SPD collection before poller.spawn() in the TUI
path so EEPROM page-cycling cannot overlap with temperature polling.

* Update error message to reflect self-healing page restore

* Fix rustfmt comment alignment

author: Patrick Buckley
message:
Fix sparkline gradient on non-TrueColor terminals (#48)

* Fix sparkline gradient not applying on non-TrueColor terminals

The RGB gradient in sparkline_color was gated behind ColorLevel::TrueColor,
causing Color256 and Basic terminals to fall through to a fixed single color
per category. Per-core CPU heatmap blocks were all the same cyan regardless
of utilization.

Add proper tiered color support:
- TrueColor: 24-bit RGB gradients (unchanged)
- Color256: map RGB to nearest xterm 6x6x6 cube index (16-231)
- Basic (16): step through dark/light ANSI color pairs
- Monochrome: grayscale ramp via 232-255 indexed palette

Extract shared gradient_rgb() to keep TrueColor and Color256 paths
in sync. Use correct non-linear xterm cube levels (0,95,135,175,215,255)
in rgb_to_cube rather than linear approximation.

* Address Copilot review: respect NO_COLOR and use theme colors for Basic

- ColorLevel::None returns Color::Reset instead of indexed grayscale,
  honoring NO_COLOR semantics
- sparkline_basic takes &self and derives colors from the active theme's
  panel colors instead of hardcoding ANSI variants

author: Patrick Buckley
message:
Unify DDR5 board probing, restructure board directory, add requirements system (#46)

Add DDR5 SPD EEPROM reader, temperature sensors, and board quirk system

DDR5 direct I2C probing via DesignWare buses (bypasses AMD FCH SPD mux):
- SPD EEPROM reader: 1024-byte page-select reads with robust page-0 restore
- Per-DIMM temperature sensors: Hub (SPD5118), TS0/TS1 (TS5111)
- SPD parser: timings, CAS latencies, JEDEC manufacturer, part number
- SPD-to-SMBIOS matching by serial number
- TUI DIMM view with live sparkline temperatures (press 'm')

Board quirk system:
- Restructure board files to vendor/chipset/board.rs hierarchy
- Unify DDR5 I2C topology into BoardTemplate via Ddr5BusConfig
- FeatureRequirements map for per-board prerequisites (BIOS version, settings)
- Post-probe diagnostics with actionable hints on failure
- Boards opt in to DDR5 probing via ddr5_bus_config field

Safety:
- MR0 device identity verified before any register writes
- Page-restore failure logged at error level
- All DDR5 paths gated behind --direct-io flag
- Sensor IDs keyed by I2C bus/addr for deterministic DIMM matching
- Cached file descriptors for I2C polling (eliminates per-poll open/close)

Supported boards: ASUS WRX90E-SAGE (BIOS >= 1317)

Co-authored-by: wendelltron <6278963+wendelltron@users.noreply.github.com>
